meta.description: Calculate body surface area (BSA) using Du Bois, Mosteller, Haycock, and Boyd formulas. Essential for chemotherapy dosing and clinical assessments. Free.

bsa.title: Body Surface Area Calculator
bsa.description: Calculate body surface area (BSA) using multiple validated formulas — essential for drug dosing and clinical assessments.
bsa.comparisonNote: All four formulas with your measurements:
bsa.contextDrugText: Chemotherapy agents and other drugs are commonly dosed by body surface area (mg/m²) to prevent under- or overdosing across patients of different sizes.
bsa.contextBurnText: BSA is the foundation of burn severity assessment (Rule of Nines) and fluid resuscitation calculations in burn patients.
bsa.contextRenalText: Glomerular filtration rate (GFR) is normalized to a standard BSA of 1.73 m² to allow comparison across patients of different body sizes.
bsa.formulaDuboisDesc: Oldest and most widely used formula. Standard in chemotherapy dosing.
bsa.formulaMostellerDesc: Simplest calculation, very accurate approximation. Recommended by the New England Journal of Medicine.
bsa.formulaHaycockDesc: Optimized for children and adults. Considered highly accurate.
bsa.formulaBoydDesc: Accounts for non-linear relationship between weight and BSA. Less commonly used.
